intro: Airship and CleverTap both show up under the push in app messaging category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Airship starts at $9; CleverTap starts at $75. Lock-in is low for Airship and high for CleverTap. Transparency lands at 2/5 versus 3/5. Both tools target mobile app engagement, so the choice comes down to pricing, lock-in, and operational fit rather than scope. Worth noting: Airship is explicitly not for web-only products without mobile. The honest trade-off: Airship trades off on web-only products without mobile; CleverTap trades off on web-only products without mobile. On the plus side, Airship highlights mobile app engagement. Airship's documentation also calls out user lifecycle messaging. For teams evaluating push in app messaging options today, the decision usually hinges on whether Airship's profile or CleverTap's profile maps more cleanly to the pricing model, lock-in tolerance, and scale your team can live with. Beyond the headline pitch, a documented Airship use-case is "User lifecycle messaging", and a documented CleverTap use-case is "User lifecycle messaging".
quick_take: Airship is for mobile app engagement; CleverTap is for mobile app engagement; decide on entry price.
verdict_a: Choose Airship if your project is mobile app engagement, a subscription starting at $9 fits your budget, you want to keep future migration cheap.
verdict_b: Choose CleverTap if your project is mobile app engagement, a subscription starting at $75 fits your budget, you are willing to accept the high lock-in called out in our data.

faq.a: Airship uses subscription from $9, and CleverTap uses subscription from $75. On published entry price, Airship lists $9 while CleverTap lists $75 — but the real cost at your scale will depend on actual usage, not just the starting tier.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Airship to CleverTap?
faq.a: Our data puts Airship at low lock-in (audiences and channels bound to airship data model), and CleverTap at high lock-in (proprietary engagement platform). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.
Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis. CleverTap does edge ahead on pricing/docs transparency (3/5 vs 2/5), which can make evaluation faster.

faq.q: Is CleverTap a good alternative to Airship?
faq.a: CleverTap covers a similar scope to Airship — our data lists "Mobile" as the best-for on both. One caveat: CleverTap is explicitly not for web-only products without mobile, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
